page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-88 is a memory-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 88 virtual CPUs mapped to 44 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 704 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 73262.01 and a PassMark CPU score of 74040.34, both placing in the top 25% tier. Memory subsystem performance is robust, demonstrating a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 5996.27 GB/sec and an uncached large block read bandwidth of 224.4 GB/sec. The processor's Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions contribute to top-tier performance in image processing and small-model LLM prompt processing, which achieved 3608.52 tokens/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-highmem-88 offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. However, single-core performance remains average, as shown by a single-core Geekbench score of 1712.0. The lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. This configuration is highly suited for large-scale database operations, high-throughput Redis deployments, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-88 is a memory-optimized server featuring 88 vCPUs and 704 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8481C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture with 44 physical cores, it delivers strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by a PassMark CPU score of 74040.34 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 73262.01. The platform lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but excels in memory-intensive tasks, showing a cached read bandwidth of 5996.27 GB/sec. It features advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512, which drive top-tier performance in image processing and prompt processing for large language models. This instance is optimized for memory-bound enterprise workloads, including large database operations, Redis caching layers, and multi-threaded software compilation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-88 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 88 vCPUs, 704 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong multi-core performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-88 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 88 vCPUs, 704 GB RAM, and Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized server featuring eighty-eight virtual cores and advanced matrix extensions for demanding enterprise database and memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 88 vCPUs and 704 GB of RAM providing a high 8 GB per vCPU memory ratio
Intel Xeon 8481C processor with AMX and AVX-512 hardware acceleration
Strong multi-core performance and high-speed memory read bandwidth up to 5996 GB/sec
Optimized for large-scale database operations, Redis caching, and software compilation
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
